PREREGISTERED_decision_rules.honesty: These are NEGATIVE/LIMIT results reported as honest nulls/characterizations. Weighting-insufficiency is the expected behavior of covariate-shift conformal under concept shift and is NOT tuned away. Verdict scores the COMMITTED pipeline (frozen split-conformal + published Tibshirani weighting), not a surrogate. No thresholds are moved after scoring.
PREREGISTERED_decision_rules.recalibration_necessary: TRUE iff shift_type in {concept, mixed} AND the cached recalibration curve restores nominal coverage. Report the minimum n_cal from the budget curve.
PREREGISTERED_decision_rules.shift_type: 'covariate' iff weighting_repairs=='yes'; 'concept' iff weighting_repairs=='no' AND matched-X KS rejects equality (p<0.001) AND recalibration on Norman's own split restores coverage; 'mixed' iff weighting_repairs=='partial' (both mechanisms contribute).
PREREGISTERED_decision_rules.weighting_repairs: 'yes' iff Tibshirani weighted-conformal coverage lands within +/-0.05 of nominal at ALL three levels; 'partial' iff within +/-0.05 at some-but-not-all levels OR all within +/-0.10; 'no' iff it misses +/-0.10 at >=2 levels (i.e. weighting leaves coverage still collapsed).

PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_a_reproduce: Reproduce frozen-fail (0.048/0.076/0.171) and recalib-restore (500-split mean ~0.755/0.864/0.927). Already verified before prereg; restated for receipt.
PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_b_covariate_diagnostic: Fit a domain classifier (HistGradientBoostingClassifier, 3-fold out-of-fold) separating Marson calibration X (label 0) from Norman X (label 1). Report OOF AUC (covariate-shift magnitude). Estimate density ratio w(x)=p(test|x)/(1-p(test|x))*prior and compute effective sample size ESS=(sum w)^2/sum(w^2) over Marson calibration points.
PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_b_oracle_bound: Compute the best-case (oracle) covariate-weighting ceiling = max Marson |residual| among calibration points inside Norman's covariate support; report fraction of Norman residuals exceeding it (points NO covariate reweighting can ever cover).
PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_b_weighted_conformal: Apply Tibshirani et al. 2019 weighted split-conformal: per Norman test point, weighted quantile of Marson |residual| using w(x). Report coverage at 0.80/0.90/0.95. This is the CONFIRMATORY covariate-shift repair test.
PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_c_concept_test: At MATCHED covariate support (Marson-Rest points with log_baseMean <= Norman max), compare Marson vs Norman |residual| distributions via two-sample KS. Concept shift <=> distributions differ at matched X (KS reject) AND weighting fails to repair.
PROCEDURE_confirmatory.step_d_budget: From cached 6-fraction x 500-split curve, report the minimum local calibration count n_cal whose 500-split coverage brackets nominal at all three levels.

cached_inputs_frozen.features_X: [log_baseMean_ontarget, cond_Rest, cond_Stim8hr, cond_Stim48hr]; Norman all cond=Rest
cached_inputs_frozen.marson_calib_residuals: reconstructed from data/interim/lodo_surface_cache.pkl (n=29279, |resid| mean 0.1173 sd 0.1126) -- reproduces frozen_q to 5 decimals (VERIFIED before this prereg)
cached_inputs_frozen.norman_residuals: handoff/norman_resid_frozen.npy (n=105, |resid| mean 0.6044 sd 0.2951); frozen coverage 0.0476/0.0762/0.1714 (VERIFIED reproduces receipt before this prereg)

check: WIN-2 cross-assay conformal transfer FAILURE DECOMPOSITION (covariate vs concept shift)
framing: CHARACTERIZATION of PUBLISHED methods (split-conformal; Tibshirani/Barber/Candes/Ramdas 2019 covariate-shift weighted conformal). NOT a new method. Reports an honest negative/limit result on cross-assay transfer.
predicted_outcome_stated_before_scoring: Exploratory (pre-confirmatory) diagnostics already indicate concept-shift dominance (Norman |resid| median 6.3x Marson at matched X; oracle covariate ceiling exceeded by ~22% of Norman points). Confirmatory weighted-conformal is expected to return weighting_repairs='no', shift_type='concept'. Pre-registering this prediction so a contrary result would be reported honestly.
question: When frozen Marson T1D CD4+ conformal quantiles fail on Norman K562 (coverage 0.048/0.076/0.171 vs nominal 0.80/0.90/0.95), is the failure COVARIATE shift (only P(X) changes; repairable by Tibshirani-style density-ratio reweighting of the calibration residuals) or CONCEPT shift (the residual distribution P(|Y-f(X)| | X) itself changes across assays; NOT repairable by covariate reweighting)?
